course_title: Fundamental MRI of the Brain & Spine
course_description:
Theoretical and practical considerations required to perform a basic MR examination of the brain and spine.

This course familiarises students with the basic techniques used to perform an MR examination of the brain and spine.

On completion of this course, you will be able to recognise normal anatomy and some common pathologies, be able to tailor the examination accordingly, and to crticially evaluate the final images.ﾠ You will also be aware of appropriate patient care.

restrictions: Enrolment restricted to Master of Magnetic Resonance program suite students.
Another instance of the same course
This course offers external and internal delivery.

course_aims: The purpose of this course is to familiarise students with theoretical and practical considerations required to perform a basic Magnetic Resonance (MR) examination of the brain and spine. Students will be able to recognise normal anatomy and some more common pathologies, be able to tailor the examination accordingly, and to critically evaluate the final images. Students will also be aware of appropriate patient care.

learning_outcomes.description: recognise normal anatomy and some common pathologies
tailor a clinical MRI examination accordingly
be aware of appropriate patient care.

assessment_details.title: Online Quiz 1
assessment_details.task_description: Description 10 Question (total 10 marks) Instructions Quiz questions are True/False, Multiple Choice, Multiple Answer or Short Answer, and are worth marks according to difficulty. Duration The quiz duration is 30 minutes (from the start of the quiz). This test is available from 13 August 9:00 to 14:00 16 August. This test can’t be saved. Once the quiz is started, it must to be completed within 30 minutes. The quiz will be submitted automatically once the time expires. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Assignment One - (Individual)
assessment_details.task_description: Video recording of PPT slides Section 1 is a Brain case study (case will be provided by the course coordinator). Section 2 is a Spine case study (case will be provided by the course coordinator). Both cases must be answered Maximum time for Video presentation is 5 minutes Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Assignments must submitted through BlackBoard (learn.uq.edu.au ). Click on https://web.library.uq.edu.au/library-services/it/learnuq-blackboard-help/kaltura-media/submit-video-or-audio-assignment-kaltura for more information on how to submit video assignment
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
See ADDITIONAL ASSESSMENT INFORMATION for the extension and deferred examination information relating to this assessment item.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
You are required to submit assessable items on time. If you fail to meet the submission deadline for any assessment item then the listed penalty will be deducted per day for up to 7 calendar days, at which point any submission will not receive any marks unless an extension has been approved. Each 24-hour block is recorded from the time the submission is due.

assessment_details.title: Final Examination
assessment_details.task_description: The examination is invigilated online exam through blackboard. You will have 10 minutes planning time followed by 1 hour to complete the exam. A further 15 minutes are allowed during which you must upload your exam to Blackboard. You will be advised of the date and time in which to complete the examination once the examination timetable has been determined. Note that late penalties will be strictly applied as per university policy. Read full task description Hide full task description

Module 2 Brain Common indications Study Module 2: Common Indications
Module 3 and Module 4 Module 3: Patient Preparation, and Module 4: Brain Protocols and Parameters
Practical Session 1 - Brain (Practical) One hour scanning session with human participant - 3T scanner
Module 5 Brain Image Evaluation Brain Image Evaluation
Practical Session 2 - Brain (Practical) One hour scanning session with human participant - 3T scanner
Module 6 Anatomy of the spine 6: Anatomy of the Spine, and complete the tutorial
Module 7 Common Indications - Spine Module 7: Common indications: Spine,
Module 8 Spine protocols and parameters Study Module 8: Spine protocols and parameters
Practical Session 3 - Spine One hour scanning session with human participant - 3T scanner
Module 9 Spine Image Evaluations Study Module 9: Spine Image Evaluations
Brain and Spine clinical case studies
